context.description: Philosophical prose on unconscious mechanisms shaping self-image and the cycle of self-definition. It critiques psychology while conceding its role in easing suffering, and introduces the section "Das Problem der Selbstdefinition".
normalization_notes: Translated the section title from German into English summary language while preserving the original title field value.

rows.paragraph_text: Unconscious mechanisms shape thought, action, and the self-image built from them, creating a self-reinforcing cycle driven by consistency and self-interest.
The text links this inner conflict to the wanderer figure, who is pulled between doubt, repression, terror, and forgetting.
It critiques modern psychology while acknowledging its value in easing suffering and treating the wounded psyche.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2. Section title: Das Problem der Selbstdefinition. Philosophical discussion of unconscious self-dynamics, self-interest, and a qualified critique of psychology.
